steps.name: Daily cap check before each call + cost.cap_reached event (BR-1, BR-2)
Wire up tests + verify all Acceptance Criteria

decisions: 2026-07-17 reconciliation: main has no cost-tracking implementation. Real work (services/cost_service.py, cost_guard.py, core/cap_guard.py, events/cost.py, api/admin/costs.py, config/pricing.yaml) exists only in worker-B's unpushed worktree. origin/feat/3-5-cost-tracking-daily-cap-man-providers exists but its diff vs. current main looks stale, not the real work. Blocked pending worker-B splitting/pushing.

2026-07-17 (later): by session start, cost_service.py + provider_status_service.py (steps 1,3-5) were already merged to main by an earlier reconciliation pass -- this state file was stale. Fixed (branch feat/3-5-cost-service-provider-status-fix, commit 23db957, merged to main at bfcc5b0): test_cost_service.py's mock session factory made `.all()`/`.one_or_none()` AsyncMock, but real SQLAlchemy async Result objects expose those as plain sync methods (only `session.execute()` itself is awaited) -- fixed to MagicMock; a generator expression building `execute_result.one_or_none`'s return value called `r.get("cost", 0.0)` on plain Row test-double objects (no `.get()`), evaluated eagerly at fixture-construction time -- fixed to `getattr(r, "cost", 0.0)`; the test's Row double only had `.grp/.cnt/.tok_in/.tok_out/.cost` but CostService.query() reads `.ti/.to` (matching real SQL `.label("ti")`/`.label("to")`) and today_by_provider() reads `.provider/.calls` (raw column names, no aliasing) -- added those as aliases on the same Row double rather than forcing two different row shapes; `CostService(session_factory=cm)` passed an already-invoked session CM directly where the service expects a zero-arg callable (`self._sf()` is called internally) -- wrapped every call site as `lambda: cm`. test_provider_status_service.py: imported `_REGISTRY` (doesn't exist, real name is lowercase `_registry`) -- fixed import; `self._settings.__getattr__ = lambda...` tried to set an unsupported magic method on a MagicMock instance -- replaced with a tiny real `_FakeSettings` class (same pattern as test_router.py's FakeSettings); `register_llm(name, cls)` was called as a plain 2-arg function but it's actually a decorator factory (`register_llm(name)` returns a decorator, so `register_llm(name)(cls)`) -- fixed all 3 call sites.

NOT fixed (out of scope for this fix pass, flagged for follow-up): Step 2 (daily cap check / cost.cap_reached event) -- app/services/cost_guard.py and app/core/cap_guard.py DO exist on main, but there is no tests/**/*cost_guard* or *cap_guard* test file at all, so this step's AC coverage is unverified either way; and Step 6 (Admin > Providers tab UI, not verified in a real browser this session per rules/testing.md).
